# @yskomur/ts-journald

> TypeScript and Node.js logging library for systemd journald with structured logging, syslog priorities, automatic backend selection, explicit `console`/`dummy` fallback modes, and compatibility with apps such as SvelteKit, Express, Fastify, and NestJS.

Use this package when a Node.js service needs one logging API that can write to journald through the optional `@yskomur/node-sdjournal` backend on Linux systemd hosts, send JSON to a managed HTTP endpoint in cloud environments, or use an explicit `console` or `dummy` fallback when no runtime backend is available.

Important notes:
- Default `backend: "auto"` chooses `journald`, then `managed`; if neither is available, configure `fallback: "console"` or `fallback: "dummy"`.
- Global helper functions create a default singleton logger instance.
- Call `close()` during shutdown if you manage logger lifecycle explicitly.
- Works best for Linux services, background workers, APIs, and SSR apps running under systemd.

## Docs

- [README](https://github.com/yskomur/ts-journald/blob/main/README.MD): English package overview, installation, API, and examples.
- [README-TR](https://github.com/yskomur/ts-journald/blob/main/README-TR.MD): Turkish package overview, API, and examples.
- [npm package](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@yskomur/ts-journald): Published package entry for installation and version discovery.

## Common tasks

- [Quick Start](https://github.com/yskomur/ts-journald/blob/main/README.MD#quick-start): Use global helpers or create a `Journal` instance.
- [Backend Behavior](https://github.com/yskomur/ts-journald/blob/main/README.MD#backend-behavior): Understand automatic backend selection.
- [Cloud Usage](https://github.com/yskomur/ts-journald/blob/main/README.MD#cloud-usage): Configure managed HTTP logging for cloud and serverless deployments.
- [Troubleshooting](https://github.com/yskomur/ts-journald/blob/main/README.MD#troubleshooting): Diagnose native journald backend or runtime issues.

## Optional

- [Repository](https://github.com/yskomur/ts-journald): Source code, issues, and release history.
